subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Invasive Species — Rate & Accumulation
stimulus: The number of acres affected by an invasive species in a fruit grove is modeled by a differentiable function $C$ of time $t$, where $t$ is measured in weeks and $0 \leq t \leq 4$. The rate of change of the number of acres affected is given by $C'(t) = 4te^{0.2t}$, where $C'(t)$ is measured in acres per week. At time $t = 0$, the number of acres affected is $3$.

parts.prompt: Find the average number of acres affected by the invasive species from time $t = 0$ to time $t = 4$ weeks. Show the setup for your calculations.

scoring_points.description: Correct integral setup for average value: $\frac{1}{4}\int_0^4 C(t)\,dt$, where $C(t) = 3 + \int_0^t 4u\,e^{0.2u}\,du$. Equivalently, the student correctly sets up the average value expression using the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.

required_elements: Average value expression $\frac{1}{4}\int_0^4 C(t)\,dt$ or equivalent setup using $C(t) = 3 + \int_0^t 4u\,e^{0.2u}\,du$

scoring_points.description: Correct answer: approximately $26.264$ acres (or the value obtained by correctly evaluating the integral with a calculator, accepted in range $26.263$–$26.265$).

parts.prompt: Find the time $t$, for $0 \leq t \leq 4$, when the instantaneous rate of change of $C$ equals the average rate of change of $C$ over the time interval $0 \leq t \leq 4$. Show the setup for your calculations.

scoring_points.description: Correct setup: computes the average rate of change $\frac{C(4) - C(0)}{4 - 0}$ and sets it equal to $C'(t) = 4t\,e^{0.2t}$, showing the equation to be solved. Note $C(4) = 3 + \int_0^4 4t\,e^{0.2t}\,dt \approx 3 + 82.434 \approx 85.434$, so the average rate of change $\approx \frac{85.434 - 3}{4} \approx 20.609$ acres per week.

parts.prompt: Assume that the invasive species continues to spread according to the given model for all times $t > 0$. Write a limit expression that describes the end behavior of the rate of change in the number of acres affected by the species. Evaluate this limit expression.

scoring_points.description: Correct evaluation: the limit equals $\infty$ (diverges to positive infinity), since $4t\,e^{0.2t}$ grows without bound as $t \to \infty$. Both the polynomial factor $4t$ and the exponential factor $e^{0.2t}$ grow without bound.

parts.prompt: At time $t = 4$ weeks after the invasive species appears in the fruit grove, measures are taken to counter the spread of the species. The function $A$, defined by $$A(t) = C(t) - \int_4^t 0.1\ln x\,dx,$$ models the number of acres affected by the species over the time interval $4 \leq t \leq 36$. At what time $t$, for $4 \leq t \leq 36$, does $A$ attain its maximum value? Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correctly differentiates $A(t)$ using the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: $A'(t) = C'(t) - 0.1\ln t = 4t\,e^{0.2t} - 0.1\ln t$ for $4 \leq t \leq 36$.

Correctly analyzes the sign of $A'(t)$: since $C'(t) = 4t\,e^{0.2t} > 0$ for all $t \geq 4$ and the remediation rate $0.1\ln t$ is much smaller than $4t\,e^{0.2t}$ on $[4, 36]$, concludes that $A'(t) > 0$ for all $t$ in $[4, 36]$. (At $t = 4$: $C'(4) = 16e^{0.8} \approx 35.7$ while $0.1\ln 4 \approx 0.139$, so $A'(4) > 0$; similarly at $t = 36$, $C'(36) \gg 0.1\ln 36$.)

required_elements: Concludes $A'(t) > 0$ for all $t$ in $[4, 36]$ with valid reasoning (exponential term dominates)

scoring_points.description: Correct conclusion with justification: $A$ attains its maximum value at $t = 36$ because $A'(t) > 0$ for all $t$ in $[4, 36]$, which means $A$ is strictly increasing on the entire interval, so the maximum occurs at the right endpoint.

required_elements: $t = 36$ with justification that $A$ is increasing on $[4, 36]$ (i.e., $A'(t) > 0$ throughout)
